subjects.subject_name: Department of Health and Human Services

calendar.description: House Natural Resources, Agriculture, and Environment Hearing
Senate Health and Human Services Hearing

general_provisions: This bill amends provisions related to cannabis.
hilighted_provisions: This bill:<hr><ltbullet>amends provisions related to hazardous waste and cannabinoid (hemp) disposal;<hr><ltbullet>requires industrial hemp retailers to check an individual's identification to ensure a purchaser is at least 21 years old;<hr><ltbullet>repeals video surveillance requirements for industrial hemp retailers;<hr><ltbullet>amends provisions related to unlawful acts concerning hemp products;<hr><ltbullet>exempts medical cannabis processors from obtaining an additional license to process hemp products;<hr><ltbullet>creates a fee on medical cannabis purchases for use in enforcement of various laws;<hr><ltbullet>renames the Cannabis Production Establishment and Pharmacy Licensing Advisory Board to the Specialized Product Authority Licensing Board (licensing board);<hr><ltbullet>reconstitutes the licensing board's membership;<hr><ltbullet>amends provisions related to labeling and packaging;<hr><ltbullet>modifies the licensing board's duties;<hr><ltbullet>moves control of the Qualified Patient Enterprise Fund to the Department of Agriculture and Food (UDAF);<hr><ltbullet>moves all Department of Health and Human Services duties related to the medical cannabis program to UDAF;<hr><ltbullet>allows medical cannabis processors to make cannabis products with a THC content below .3% (low THC products); <hr><ltbullet>allows medical cannabis pharmacies to sell low THC products;<hr><ltbullet>includes a coordination clause to address a cross-reference in S.B. 121, Medical Cannabis Program Amendments; and<hr><ltbullet>includes a coordination clause with H.B. 385, Specialized Product Sales Amendments, to address the movement of the Qualified Patient Enterprise Fund.
